summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates AWS credentials and host data to attacker-controlled endpoints
details: On npm install, postinstall.js runs a lifecycle script that queries the AWS instance metadata service (169.254.169.254) via curl to harvest IAM role credentials (AccessKeyId, SecretAccessKey, SessionToken), invokes `aws sts get-caller-identity`, and iterates `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` across us-east-1, eu-west-1, and eu-central-1 to dump every accessible secret. It also collects host metadata (os.hostname(), uid, cwd) and searches the filesystem for sensitive files. The harvested data is then POSTed to a hardcoded webhook.site collection URL and PUT to attacker-controlled IPs at 154.57.164.80, 154.57.164.82, and 154.57.164.71 on ports 30543/31250/31289. The package self-describes as a 'CTF payload for verdaccio supply-chain testing' — it is an explicit supply-chain attack payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
